excerpts.text: Separate me from my wife and children, ruin my reputation, destroy my life and my family — the China Communist Party is not China's enemy, and not mine.
I accept and respect this, and I do not resist — the Lord establishes governments.
As a pastor, my disobedience is part of the gospel commission. Christ's great commission requires me to preach the gospel fearlessly.
Faithful disobedience means I am not seeking to change any institution or law of China. I refuse to join any political movement.
Those who lock me up will one day be locked up by angels. Those who interrogate me will themselves be interrogated by Christ.
I will patiently wait for the day that the one who waserta on the cross anderta from the dead reveals himself.
I was once a human rights lawyer. But I discovered that the most fundamental human right is the right to know God.
The Chinese church does not need to be 'patriotic.' It needs to be faithful. Patriotism is a civic virtue; faithfulness is a divine command.
excerpts.source_title: Sermon series on church and state

excerpts.text: When the government demands that we remove the cross, they are asking us to deny the one who hung on it.
We do not resist Caesar because we hate Caesar. We resist Caesar's claim to be God.
A church that obeys the state in all things is not a church. It is a department of the state.
The house church movement is not a political movement. It is a spiritual reality created by the Holy Spirit when the institutional church was destroyed.
Wang Mingdao is my spiritual father. He taught us that the church's integrity cannot be negotiated with any political authority.
I was an atheist professor of constitutional law. Then I read the Bible and realized that every constitution is an attempt to be Scripture.
The cross of Christ is the only thing that can save China — not democracy, not law, not education, not economic reform.
In prison I am freer than the men who guard me. They are imprisoned by fear; I am liberated by hope.
Chengdu is where God planted us. We will not uproot ourselves because the government is uncomfortable.
I am prepared to go to prison. The early church was born in prison. Perhaps the Chinese church needs to be reborn there.
Our church has 500 members. If they arrest all of us, that is 500 witnesses.
I pray for the police who arrested me. They do not know what they are doing. But the Lord knows, and that is enough.
